summary: Substantial Classic Mode decompilation work in src/melee/gm/gmclassic.c. The PR replaced several stubbed gmClassic functions with C implementations, added local data layouts for Classic matchup and intro data, refined gm_803DDEC8Struct and callback fields in gm/types.h, and made small header/prototype cleanups. decomp.dev reported 3 new matches and 4 improved unmatched items, with gmClassic_801B3B40 and gmClassic_801B3A34 reaching 100% and gmClassic_801B3500, gmClassic_OnLoad, gmClassic_801B2D54, and gmClassic_801B2BA4 improving substantially but not all fully matching.
what_changed: Implemented gmClassic_801B2BA4 as a static matchup-selection helper using gmMainLib_8015CDC8()->c_kind, gm_80164430, gm_80164840, Stage_8022519C, and existing gm_803DDEC8Struct entries to avoid invalid or duplicate character/stage matchups.
Added static inline gmClassic_InitMatchupOrder to count matchup arrays until stage sentinel 0x148, initialize an order array, and shuffle it with HSD_Randi.

Implemented gmClassic_801B2D54 as a static helper assigning gm_803DDEC8Struct.xC matchup pointers from typed gmClassic_803DDEC8 data ranges based on x1 flags, including special static gmClassicMatchupData entries gm_804D4318, gm_804D4320, and gm_804D4328.

Implemented gmClassic_OnLoad to clear existing Classic matchup pointers, randomize matchup order buffers in gmClassic_80490880, initialize the 1P/All-Star-like state through gm_8017EB30 and gm_8017C984, zero gm_804908A0 result state bytes, install callback function pointers, set minor scene 0x70, and call gm_80172174 and Ground_801C5A28.

Implemented gmClassic_801B3500 to build Classic intro/preload state, count allies and enemies, set enemy colors with gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable, configure partner/enemy data through gm_8017DB88, populate lbDvd game_cache character and stage entries, and request audio preload bits through lbAudioAx functions.

Implemented gmClassic_801B3A34 to prepare StartMeleeData for a Classic match, including special target-test and team-stage stage selection, storing flags/progress in UnkAllstarData, and calling gm_8017CE34 and gm_8016F088.

Implemented gmClassic_801B3B40 to process match exit info, record per-stage result state in gm_804908A0, handle clear progression, and update target-test records/unlocks using gmMainLib and Ground timing helpers.
Introduced local gmclassic.c structs gmClassicMatchup, gmClassicMatchupData, gmClassicIntroData, gmClassic_80490880Data, and gmClassic_803DDEC8Data with STATIC_ASSERT size checks.
Refined struct UnkAllstarData and UnkAdventureData callback fields in types.h from UNK_T/u32 placeholders to typed function pointers.
Reworked gm_803DDEC8Struct in types.h from bitfields and opaque padding to explicit bytes/halfwords and a struct gmClassicMatchup* xC pointer.

Removed non-public prototypes and global declaration from gmclassic.h for gmClassic_801B2BA4, gmClassic_801B2D54, and gmClassic_803DDEC8 after those symbols became local/static or were represented via typed externs in gmclassic.c.
Changed gmallstar.c assignment data->x58 = 0 to data->x58 = NULL, consistent with x58 now being a function pointer.
smart_moves: Used file-local structs with exact offset comments and STATIC_ASSERTs to describe anonymous Classic data blobs without over-exporting uncertain types.
Forward-declared struct gmClassicMatchup in types.h so gm_803DDEC8Struct could carry a typed pointer while the detailed matchup layout stayed local to gmclassic.c.
Converted callback slots in shared 1P mode structs to concrete function pointer types, enabling Classic and All-Star code to use NULL/function assignments more accurately.
Kept helper functions gmClassic_801B2BA4 and gmClassic_801B2D54 static after decompilation, reducing gmclassic.h API surface.
Used sentinel-based iteration patterns, especially x00 == 0x148 for matchup tables and x0 == 0x0D for gm_803DDEC8Struct lists, matching the observed data layout.
Matched preload behavior by explicitly constructing lbDvd game_cache entries and OR-ing character/stage audio bitmasks, rather than treating preload setup as opaque.
decomp_lessons: When a data symbol is actually a compound blob, wrapping it in a local typed aggregate can unlock pointer arithmetic and array indexing matches; this PR changed gmClassic_803DDEC8 from a single exported gm_803DDEC8Struct assumption to gmClassic_803DDEC8Data with x00, x0C0, x0CC, x1B8, x26C, and x2B0 regions.

Opaque callback fields block downstream matching; replacing UNK_T/u32 placeholders with function pointer signatures can make call sites, NULL assignments, and casts express original intent more closely.

Bitfields are not always the best representation for flag bytes in decomp work. This PR replaced gm_803DDEC8Struct.x1 bitfields with a raw u8 x1 and used explicit masks such as 2, 4, 8, 0x10, 0x20, and 0x80.

Keep uncertain or mode-specific data layouts local when possible. The detailed Classic matchup and intro structs were not promoted into shared headers, limiting churn while still improving gmclassic.c.
Use sentinel values as table boundaries instead of hardcoding counts when that matches the binary's control flow; gmClassic_InitMatchupOrder and gmClassic_801B2BA4 both recount until 0x148.

Partial but high-quality implementations are useful: decomp.dev showed full matches for gmClassic_801B3A34 and gmClassic_801B3B40, while larger functions still improved substantially and provided typed scaffolding for future cleanup.
naming_conventions: New local structs followed the existing address/data-style naming where semantics were still limited: gmClassic_80490880Data and gmClassic_803DDEC8Data.
Mode-specific semantic names were used where evidence was strong enough: gmClassicMatchup, gmClassicMatchupData, gmClassicIntroData, and gmClassic_InitMatchupOrder.
Unknown fields retained x-offset names such as x00, x02, x0C0, x1B8, x2B0 with explicit offset comments, matching project style for not-yet-understood layouts.
Helper functions that are not exported were made static/static inline rather than kept in gmclassic.h as public UNK_RET prototypes.
Pointer nullness should be expressed as NULL rather than integer 0 when a field has been typed as a callback pointer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced PAD_STACK in gmClassic_801B3A34 and gmClassic_801B3B40 to preserve stack layout for matching.
Used small scoped blocks and local variables such as ep, echar, achar, ap, audio, sp8, and spC to shape register lifetimes and stack usage.
Retained explicit casts for callback signatures passed to gm_8017DB88, e.g. u8 (*)(s32, s32, u8) and f32 (*)(s32, s32), to bridge shared type definitions with the callee prototype.
Used deliberate infinite loops for impossible no-match cases in gmClassic_801B2D54, matching apparent original trap behavior.
Used raw flag masks and explicit u8 casts around gm_803DDEC8Struct fields to match byte-oriented comparisons in the original code.
Preserved sentinel loops and repeated recounting of matchup count inside gmClassic_801B2BA4 rather than simplifying, likely helping the generated control flow stay close to the target.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported matched code increased by 784 bytes and matched data increased by 24 bytes.
decomp.dev reported 3 new full matches: gmClassic_801B3B40 (+516 bytes), gmClassic_801B3A34 (+268 bytes), and gmclassic .sdata (+24 bytes).
decomp.dev reported improvements but not full matches for gmClassic_801B3500 at 92.27%, gmClassic_OnLoad at 84.00%, gmClassic_801B2D54 at 99.96%, and gmClassic_801B2BA4 at 93.87%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching gmClassic_OnLoad or gmClassic_801B3500 after this partial implementation.
Search for subsequent renames of gmClassicMatchup fields x00/x02/x05 or gm_803DDEC8Struct fields x1/x2/x4/x6/x8/xC.
Look for PRs that move gmClassicMatchup or gmClassicIntroData out of gmclassic.c into shared headers.
Search later changes to UnkAllstarData callback field signatures x48 through x70 to confirm whether u8/f32 function pointer prototypes were refined.
Find follow-up review or cleanup around the deliberate infinite loops in gmClassic_801B2D54 no-match cases.
